steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is the average processing power (singular). Strip out the parenthetical '(now often containing dozens of cores)' — it modifies microchips, not the subject. The sentence also uses 'since then,' pointing to a span from the early 2000s to the present, which requires present-perfect tense.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'has improved': Singular present-perfect. Matches the singular subject and the since-then timeframe. ✓
  • Choice B — 'have improved': Plural present-perfect. Disagrees with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — 'have been improving': Plural present-perfect progressive. Disagrees with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'are improving': Plural simple present. Disagrees with the singular subject and does not capture the since-then span. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
